target_emits.name: create_policy_shared
target_emits.purpose: Create the policy profile in the Library. The body is structurally complete; security-policy.policy-rule[] is supplied by the preprocessing function as `_central_rules` (which it computes from acl_sess__v4policy + acl_sess__v6policy + role_records via the documented merge rule).
target_emits.iteration_notes: One POST per ACL. The policy-rule[] array is pre-computed by the preprocessing function before key_mappings run.

target_emits.name: assign_policy
target_emits.purpose: Assign the policy profile to the target scope. Multi-device-function array packed into a single POST.

config-assignment: {one entry per device_function in target_meta.device_functions: { scope-id, device-function, profile-type='policies', profile-instance='{name}' }}

target_emits.iteration_notes: One POST; all device-functions share the array.

target_meta.device_function_filtering_rule: Per the operator's 'x-supportedDeviceType is field-honored, not body-gated' clarification: a single policy body works across all device-functions. Each device honors only the fields applicable to it. For AOS 8 -> Central GW migration we target MOBILITY_GW; the body shape is permissive and other device-functions could be added if AP / Switch use cases land later.

target_scope_id_resolution.rule: Central scope_id is supplied by the consuming skill at runtime, derived from the AOS 8 acl_sess record's binding scope.
target_scope_id_resolution.input: Source binding scope (string path / identifier); skill-specific

dependencies.depends_on: central:role (the roles referenced by the ACL must already exist in Central before policies referencing them are POSTed)

depended_by.field: (none — no back-reference on the role object)
depended_by.relation: The role->policy linkage is IMPLICIT, not stored on the role. Verified live: central_get_role_with_policy returns policy:null and the role record carries no policies[]. A policy binds to a role via its own policy-rule[].condition.source.role-list PLUS co-assignment of the role and the policy at the same scope/device-function.
depended_by.notes: AOS 8 has role.role__acl[] referencing ACL names; Central does NOT invert this into a role.policies[] array. Do not expect Central to back-fill a role->policies reference (issue #419). NOTE: assigning the policy ALSO requires it to be registered as a policy-group entry first — see issue #420; this spec's step 2 (direct config-assignment) is incomplete until that lands.

objects.live_shape_example: [{"accname": "missing-policies", "acl_sess__v4policy": [{"sany": true, "src": "sany", "dany": true, "dst": "dany", "echo": true, "icmp_type": "echo", "svc": "icmp", "service_app": "service", "permit": true, "action": "permit"}, {"suser": true, "src": "suser", "dipaddr": "10.1.1.1", "dst": "dhost", "service-name": "svc-http", "svc": "service-name", "service_app": "service", "dnatport": 8080, "action": "dst-nat"}, {"suser": true, "src": "suser", "dany": true, "dst": "dany", "tungrpname": "test", "re_dir": "tunnel-group", "action": "redir_opt"}], "acl_sess__v6policy": []}]
objects.notes: Live-verified across 88 user-customized ACLs in the operator's tenant + a purpose-configured 'missing-policies' ACL exercising the variants not present elsewhere. Each ACL has parallel acl_sess__v4policy and acl_sess__v6policy rule arrays; the preprocessing function merges them into a single tagged Central policy-rule[] array (each entry tagged with address-family per its source array).

Used ONLY for the role_attribution reverse-index. The preprocessing function scans role records to find which roles reference each ACL (role__acl[].pname == acl.accname AND acl_type='session'). The full role list is supplied to emit_calls via runtime_values['role_records']; the preprocessing function does the per-ACL filtering. Consumer pre-fetches roles ONCE per migration run, not per-ACL.

merge_rule.description: Engine auto-invokes hpe_networking_mcp.translations.preprocessing.aos8_policy.preprocess_acl_for_policy before key_mappings. The preprocessing function reads runtime_values['role_records'], scans for entries whose role__acl[].pname matches the current ACL's accname (with acl_type='session'), and collects the matching rname values into role_attribution. That role_attribution is then used to populate ADDRESS_ROLE.role-list when AOS 8 rules have src='suser' or dst='duser' (the implicit 'user-of-this-role' semantic) or for the any-any bidirectional expansion (role->any AND any->role).
merge_rule.inheritance_handling: Drop role records with top-level _flags.default=true (system roles like 'logon', 'guest', 'voice', etc.). Drop individual role__acl[] entries with _flags.system / _flags.readonly / _flags.default. Both filterings happen inside preprocess_acl_for_policy.

name.to: policy name (Central path param + body 'name', profile-instance on step 2)

policy_rules.notes: Pre-computed by the preprocessing function (declared in the top-level preprocessing field). Result is the full Central policy-rule[] array — engine substitutes verbatim into the body wherever {policy_rules} appears. Empty / no-rules ACLs return [] which the body keeps as an empty array; consumer should typically filter ACLs with empty rule lists from migration before calling emit_calls.

association.notes: Pre-computed by the preprocessing function. AOS 8 'validuser' (and any other interface-ACL name in _INTERFACE_ACL_NAMES) is an INTERFACE ACL → ASSOCIATION_INTERFACE; every other ACL is role-applied → ASSOCIATION_ROLE.

unmapped_fields.from: AOS 8 rules with apptosstr / appprio8021p / queue / queue_type (QoS marking)
unmapped_fields.reason: Central's QoS / packet-marking action sub-shapes (action.packet-marking, action.policing, action.qos) exist but the AOS 8 source-side mapping is not yet authored. Deferred to a v2 of this translation; the preprocessing function currently drops these fields silently.
unmapped_fields.todo: Configure AOS 8 ACLs with QoS marking + capture; add packet-marking / policing builders to _build_action.

unmapped_fields.from: AOS 8 rules with src-nat / route actions
unmapped_fields.reason: src-nat (ACTION_SOURCE_NAT) and route (ACTION_ROUTE / PBR) actions are mapped to Central enum values but the action sub-config (source-nat.{ip,pool}, routing-action.{tunnel-group, dscp}) is not yet built by the preprocessing function. Less common than dst-nat/dual-nat/redirect; deferred to v2.

unmapped_fields.from: AOS 8 rules with mirror / blacklist / captive action options
unmapped_fields.reason: Less common action variants without verified Central body shapes; deferred to v2 once we have configured live samples.

unmapped_fields.from: AOS 8 ADDRESS_GROUP / ADDRESS_DOMAIN_NAME / ADDRESS_AP_* / ADDRESS_MASTER_IP source/destination types
unmapped_fields.reason: Central enum has 14 source/destination address types; preprocessing maps the 7 most common (ANY, HOST, NETWORK, ALIAS, ROLE, LOCAL, USER). The remaining 7 (DOMAIN_NAME, GROUP, AP_IP, AP_IP_ALL, AP_NETWORK, MASTER_IP, SUBNET_MASK) have no direct AOS 8 source-side discriminator we've verified. Add per discriminator as configured samples surface.
Operator scoped these out at translation authoring time (rarely used; better handled by alternative products). Preprocessing handles redirect tunnel + redirect tunnel-group only.

AOS 8 CLI grammar treats source-spec / destination-spec as a single token (one of: user, user-role, host, network, alias, any) — no composition. Central's ArubaPolicyCondition_SourceRoleOptions / DestinationRoleOptions is a Central-native enhancement with no AOS 8 source path. Verified live: across 88 ACLs in the operator's tenant, zero rules combine a role discriminator with an additional address constraint on the same side.

ignored_variants.reason: AOS 8 system / built-in ACLs (global-sacl, apprf-*-sacl, etc.) appear with system flags. Migrating them creates duplicate policies in Central that shadow Central's own built-ins. Preprocessing filters these from the role-attribution lookup.

ignored_variants.form: Empty acl_sess record (rule list empty after filtering)
ignored_variants.reason: Per the AOS 8 -> CNX LLD: 'Empty ACL will NOT be migrated.' Preprocessing returns an empty list for ACLs with no rules; consumer should typically filter empty ACLs from the migration set rather than POSTing a rule-less policy.

ignored_variants.form: ACL not attached to any role (role_attribution = empty list)
ignored_variants.reason: Per the LLD: 'ACL by itself will NOT be migrated.' Preprocessing falls back to ADDRESS_ANY for suser/duser rules when role_attribution is empty (rather than producing an invalid empty role-list); consumer should typically skip ACLs with no role attribution.

draft_notes: v1 translation, Gateway-targeted. POST /policies/{name} with security-policy.policy-rule[] computed by the aos8_policy preprocessing function (engine auto-invokes via the top-level preprocessing field).

TEMPLATE COMPLIANCE: this translation follows the standard Paradigm-B template (per-field key_mappings + structurally complete body). The v4policy/v6policy merge + role_attribution lookup + any-any bidirectional expansion all live in the preprocessing function (translations/preprocessing/aos8_policy.py); the JSON is thin and reviewable. See translations/AUTHORING.md for the template + decision tree.

INVERSION CALL-OUT: AOS 8 binds ACL->role via role.role__acl[] (role-side reference). Central inverts — policies reference roles via policy-rule[].condition.source.role-list (policy-side reference) and Central back-fills role.policies[] automatically. Preprocessing scans role_records (caller-supplied via runtime_values) to compute role_attribution per ACL.

Live-verified across 88 user-customized ACLs at /md/Campus/West + a purpose-configured 'missing-policies' ACL. amazon-device live Central GW policy verified RULE_PROTOCOL + ip-header.protocol works on Gateway despite schema marking it AP-only. Schema annotations on Central /policies are unreliable for Gateway support; trust live data.

ANY-ANY EXPANSION: AOS 8 'any any' is bidirectional and Central can't represent it for a role-bound policy. Preprocessing expands one AOS 8 'any any' rule into TWO Central rules (role->any AND any->role) so return traffic matches. Live-verified pattern from PD-allowall policy.

NAMED svc-* uses Central's authoritative net-service catalog: AOS 8 service-name passes verbatim into Central's services.net-service field with rule-type RULE_NET_SERVICE. Central ships ~73 net-services including 64 svc-* matching AOS 8 conventions. Unknown names produce a clean Central error rather than silent mistranslation.

Enum mappings (services.application / app-category / web-category / web-reputation) come from policy.json's x-enumDescriptions table — extracted at authoring time into translations/policy_enum_tables.py. 21/85 web-category entries insert connective words ('AND') between slash-separated AOS 8 forms (e.g. entertainment/arts -> ENTERTAINMENT-AND-ARTS) — a naive uppercase + slash-to-dash transform produces wrong values for those. Always use the explicit lookup table.

TCP/UDP+port shape inferred from schema, not live-verified: live-verification confirms RULE_PROTOCOL + ip-header.protocol works on GW. RULE_TCP / RULE_UDP with transport-fields.destination-port follow the schema's parallel structure — should be verified against the operator's tenant before treating as authoritative for production migration.
Idempotency: if a policy with the same name already exists, the SHARED POST returns HTTP 409. Consumer must handle 409 idempotently.
